source: MITRE ATT&CK Enterprise (curated subset for attack-surface mapping)

mitigations.M1035: Limit Access to Resource Over Network
mitigations.M1042: Disable or Remove Feature or Program

techniques.name: Exploit Public-Facing Application
techniques.description: Adversaries exploit a weakness in an internet-facing host or application.

techniques.name: External Remote Services
techniques.description: Adversaries leverage exposed remote services for access.

techniques.name: Valid Accounts
techniques.description: Adversaries use compromised legitimate credentials.

techniques.name: Valid Accounts: Cloud Accounts
techniques.description: Use of compromised cloud account credentials.

techniques.name: Unsecured Credentials
techniques.description: Adversaries search for insecurely stored credentials.

techniques.name: Unsecured Credentials: Credentials In Files
techniques.description: Credentials stored in files (env, config, source).

techniques.name: Unsecured Credentials: Private Keys
techniques.description: Exposed private cryptographic keys.

techniques.name: Unsecured Credentials: Cloud Instance Metadata API
techniques.description: Credential theft via cloud metadata service (often via SSRF).

techniques.name: Unsecured Credentials: Container API
techniques.description: Credential/secret access via exposed container APIs.

techniques.name: Data from Information Repositories
techniques.description: Mining information repositories for sensitive data.

techniques.name: Data from Information Repositories: Code Repositories
techniques.description: Sensitive data harvested from exposed source code repositories.

techniques.name: Data from Cloud Storage
techniques.description: Access to data from misconfigured cloud storage.

techniques.name: File and Directory Discovery
techniques.description: Enumerating files and directories.

techniques.name: Gather Victim Host Information
techniques.description: Collecting information about victim hosts.

techniques.name: Active Scanning
techniques.description: Probing infrastructure via active scans.

techniques.name: Search Open Websites/Domains
techniques.description: OSINT from public sources.

techniques.name: Gather Victim Identity Information
techniques.description: Collecting identity information (emails, accounts).

techniques.name: Steal Web Session Cookie
techniques.description: Theft of web session cookies for account access.

techniques.name: Forge Web Credentials
techniques.description: Forging authentication material such as cookies or tokens.

techniques.name: Forge Web Credentials: Web Cookies
techniques.description: Forging web session cookies (e.g., weak JWT signing).

techniques.name: Steal Application Access Token
techniques.description: Theft of OAuth/application access tokens.

techniques.name: Modify Authentication Process
techniques.description: Tampering with authentication mechanisms.

techniques.name: Brute Force
techniques.description: Credential guessing / account enumeration without rate limits.

techniques.name: Command and Scripting Interpreter: JavaScript
techniques.description: Execution of attacker JavaScript (e.g., via XSS).

techniques.name: Phishing
techniques.description: Phishing, including via abused open redirects.

techniques.name: User Execution
techniques.description: Reliance on user action to execute attacker content.

techniques.name: Drive-by Compromise
techniques.description: Compromise via browsing, aided by missing client-side protections.

techniques.name: Adversary-in-the-Middle
techniques.description: Interception enabled by weak transport security.

techniques.name: Deploy Container
techniques.description: Deploying containers via exposed orchestration/registry APIs.

techniques.name: Container and Resource Discovery
techniques.description: Discovering containers/resources via exposed APIs.

techniques.name: Server Software Component: Web Shell
techniques.description: Persistence via web shells on compromised apps.

techniques.name: Exfiltration Over Web Service
techniques.description: Exfiltration of data over web services.

techniques.name: Network Denial of Service
techniques.description: Network-level denial of service.

techniques.name: Endpoint Denial of Service
techniques.description: Application/endpoint denial of service (e.g., no rate limiting).
